agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "New 'Express Delivery' product offering faster delivery with a time-window commitment, premium pricing, and conditional refund policy. This is an upsell on top of a standard delivery service within an existing courier/logistics platform.",
    "bounded_context": "Express Delivery — a specialized fulfillment mode within the Order Fulfillment bounded context, intersecting with Pricing, Driver Assignment, and Customer Communication contexts.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Express Delivery", "definition": "A premium delivery option promising a 2-hour delivery window from order placement, available to customers within 10km of a warehouse." },
      { "term": "Express Fee", "definition": "A fixed premium surcharge ($12.99) added on top of the base delivery fee when a customer selects express delivery." },
      { "term": "Base Delivery Fee", "definition": "The standard delivery charge applied to all orders regardless of delivery speed tier." },
      { "term": "Delivery Window", "definition": "The 2-hour time period starting from order placement within which an express delivery is expected to be completed." },
      { "term": "Pickup SLA", "definition": "The internal operational requirement that an express order must be picked up from the warehouse within 15 minutes of order placement." },
      { "term": "Missed Window", "definition": "A delivery that was not completed within the 2-hour delivery window, triggering eligibility for an express fee refund." },
      { "term": "Express-Eligible Driver", "definition": "A driver with a rating of 4.0 stars or higher who is qualified to fulfill express deliveries." },
      { "term": "Replacement Assignment", "definition": "The process of assigning a new driver when the original express driver cancels mid-delivery, subject to a 10-minute reassignment window." },
      { "term": "Goodwill Refund", "definition": "A non-contractual refund of the express fee issued when the delivery window is missed, subject to exclusions (e.g., wrong customer address)." },
      { "term": "Warehouse Proximity", "definition": "The 10km radius from a warehouse that determines whether a customer's delivery address is eligible for express delivery." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "Express delivery is only available to customers whose delivery address is within 10km of an active warehouse.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Physical constraint — a 2-hour window is unachievable beyond this radius. Offering express outside this range would guarantee failure and refund liability.",
      "testable_assertion": "The system must reject express delivery selection when the delivery address is farther than 10km from the nearest active warehouse."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "The express fee is a fixed $12.99 surcharge added on top of the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Pricing is a business policy that can be changed by Finance or Product. The amount is not a physical or data-integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a customer selects express delivery, the order total must include the base delivery fee plus exactly $12.99."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "An express order must be picked up from the warehouse within 15 minutes of order placement.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational policy threshold. Operations could adjust this to 10 or 20 minutes without corrupting system state. However, it directly impacts the feasibility of BR-5.",
      "testable_assertion": "If no driver has picked up an express order within 15 minutes of placement, the system must escalate the order (specific escalation action TBD — see escalations)."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "Only drivers with a rating of 4.0 stars or above are eligible to be assigned express deliveries.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Quality threshold policy. Operations could change the cutoff. However, assigning a sub-4-star driver to express violates the current eligibility rule.",
      "testable_assertion": "The driver assignment system must never assign an express delivery to a driver whose current rating is below 4.0 stars."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "The express delivery window is 2 hours from order placement. A delivery completed after this window is classified as a missed window.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "This is the core time-boundary that defines the express product. If the window definition is inconsistent or mutable mid-delivery, refund logic, SLA tracking, and customer communication all break. The window duration itself could change as policy, but the invariant is: once set at order placement, it must not be altered.",
      "testable_assertion": "The delivery window expiry timestamp must be set to exactly order_placed_at + 2 hours and must not be modified after order placement."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "When a delivery window is missed, the express fee ($12.99) is refunded but the base delivery fee is not refunded.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Data integrity — a refund that includes the base fee would violate the financial model. The refund amount must be exactly the express surcharge, no more, no less.",
      "testable_assertion": "A missed-window refund must equal exactly the express fee charged on that order, and must not include any portion of the base delivery fee."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "No refund is issued if the customer provided an incorrect delivery address, even if the window is missed.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Policy-level exclusion. Customer Success or Legal could add or remove exclusion reasons.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a missed-window event occurs and the order is flagged as 'incorrect_address', the system must not issue an automatic refund."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "Missed-window refunds are processed within 3 business days.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational SLA for Finance processing, not a system integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "A refund initiated for a missed window must have a processing deadline of no more than 3 business days from the missed-window event."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-9",
      "rule": "If an express driver cancels mid-delivery, a replacement driver must be assigned within 10 minutes.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational policy threshold. The 10-minute window is a target, not a data-integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "When an express driver cancels, the system must initiate replacement assignment immediately and track elapsed time against a 10-minute threshold."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-10",
      "rule": "If no replacement driver is assigned within 10 minutes of a mid-delivery cancellation, the express fee is automatically refunded.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Automatic refund trigger is a policy decision. The threshold and auto-refund behavior are business choices.",
      "testable_assertion": "When 10 minutes elapse after a driver cancellation without a replacement being assigned, the system must automatically initiate an express fee refund."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-11",
      "rule": "Express delivery customers must have access to real-time driver tracking.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Feature requirement, not a data integrity or physical constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "When an express order is in transit, the customer must be able to view the assigned driver's location updating at intervals no greater than 30 seconds (interval TBD — see escalations)."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Customer is exactly 10km from the warehouse boundary", "expected_behavior": "Boundary condition — must define whether 10km is inclusive or exclusive. Recommend inclusive (<=10km is eligible)." },
    { "scenario": "Driver rating drops below 4.0 stars while en route on an express delivery", "expected_behavior": "Must not cancel in-flight delivery. Rating eligibility is checked at assignment time only, not during transit." },
    { "scenario": "No express-eligible drivers are available at order time", "expected_behavior": "Express option should not be offered to the customer, or order should be rejected with a clear message. Must not accept an express order that cannot be fulfilled." },
    { "scenario": "Customer changes address after placing an express order", "expected_behavior": "Undefined. If new address is >10km, does the order lose express status? Is the fee refunded? If the window is missed due to address change, does the wrong-address exclusion apply?" },
    { "scenario": "Replacement driver also cancels mid-delivery", "expected_behavior": "Undefined. Does the 10-minute clock reset? Is there a cap on replacement attempts? At what point is the delivery failed entirely?" },
    { "scenario": "Order is placed at 11:55 PM — 2-hour window spans midnight and crosses into a non-business day for refund processing", "expected_behavior": "The delivery window itself is absolute clock time and unaffected. Refund processing SLA of 3 business days starts from the missed-window event regardless of when it occurs." },
    { "scenario": "Warehouse goes offline or runs out of inventory after express order is placed", "expected_behavior": "Undefined. Is this a missed window? A cancellation? Who initiates the refund and what is refunded?" },
    { "scenario": "Multiple warehouses within 10km — which one is selected?", "expected_behavior": "Undefined. Nearest? Most available? This affects pickup SLA feasibility." },
    { "scenario": "Driver picks up at minute 14 (within 15-min SLA) but delivery window is already tight", "expected_behavior": "Pickup SLA is met. Delivery window is still measured from order placement, not pickup. Late pickup compresses the transit window but does not extend the 2-hour guarantee." },
    { "scenario": "Customer Success flags 'wrong address' after auto-refund has already been issued", "expected_behavior": "Undefined. Can a goodwill refund be clawed back? This has legal and UX implications." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Express fee ($12.99/order) is a new revenue stream. Refund exposure is significant if missed-window rates are high — each miss is a 100% loss on the express surcharge with fulfillment costs still incurred. The auto-refund on failed driver replacement adds uncontrolled refund liability. No cap on refund volume is defined.",
    "operational": "Constraining express to 4-star+ drivers reduces the eligible driver pool, potentially creating supply bottlenecks during peak hours. The 15-minute pickup SLA requires tight dispatch orchestration. The 10-minute replacement window during cancellations demands a hot-standby driver pool or priority queue mechanism.",
    "user": "Real-time tracking and a prominent time commitment set high customer expectations. The contradiction between 'guarantee' messaging and 'best effort' legal posture creates a trust risk if customers feel misled. Refund exclusions (wrong address) may generate disputes.",
    "risk": "The guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction is the highest risk — marketing a 'guarantee' that Legal says cannot be guaranteed in writing exposes the company to false advertising claims. Additionally, the auto-refund on replacement failure creates a vector for refund fraud (driver-customer collusion on cancellations).",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "New premium option with high expectations. Satisfaction depends on the gap between 'guarantee' language and actual delivery rates. Wrong-address refund exclusion may frustrate legitimate customers." },
      { "group": "Drivers", "impact": "4-star+ requirement creates a tiered driver class. Express drivers face tighter time pressure. Cancellation triggers immediate replacement, adding performance pressure." },
      { "group": "Warehouse Operations", "impact": "15-minute pickup SLA requires express orders to be prioritized in pick/pack workflows. May disrupt standard order throughput." },
      { "group": "Finance", "impact": "New refund processing workflow within 3 business days. Must track express fee separately from base fee for partial refund accuracy. Auto-refund triggers add unplanned liability." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Will handle disputes around wrong-address exclusions, near-miss windows, and the guarantee-vs-best-effort confusion. Need clear scripts and tooling." },
      { "group": "Legal/Compliance", "impact": "Must review all customer-facing language to ensure no enforceable guarantee is implied. Refund policy must be framed as goodwill, not obligation." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 38,
    "reasoning": "The spec contains a direct, unresolved contradiction between Product/Customer Success ('guaranteed 2 hours, shown prominently') and Legal ('cannot guarantee in writing, best effort only'). This is not a terminology quibble — it fundamentally changes the product's identity, customer-facing language, refund obligation framing, and potential legal exposure. Until this is resolved, acceptance criteria cannot be finalized because we do not know what promise we are making to the customer. Additionally, several operational flows (escalation on missed pickup SLA, replacement driver failure chains, address change handling) are undefined.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Express fee amount and refund scope (fee only, not base) are clearly defined",
      "10km warehouse proximity eligibility is a clear, testable constraint",
      "4-star driver eligibility threshold is unambiguous",
      "15-minute pickup SLA is clearly stated"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Nature of the 2-hour commitment: guarantee vs. best effort — this is a showstopper contradiction",
      "Customer-facing language for the time commitment",
      "Escalation behavior when pickup SLA is breached",
      "Replacement driver failure chain behavior",
      "Wrong-address determination process and dispute handling",
      "Whether auto-refund on replacement failure interacts with wrong-address exclusion"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The 10km distance is calculated as straight-line (geodesic) distance, not driving distance — this needs confirmation",
      "Driver rating is the rolling average visible in the driver profile, not a per-delivery score",
      "Order placement time is the timestamp when the order is confirmed and payment captured, not when the cart is created",
      "Express delivery is for a single warehouse-to-customer leg, not multi-hop",
      "The 2-hour window is wall-clock time and does not pause for any reason"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "CRITICAL: Product Manager and Customer Success describe the 2-hour window as a 'guarantee' to be 'shown prominently.' Legal explicitly states this cannot be guaranteed in writing and is 'best effort' only. These are mutually exclusive positions. The entire product's customer-facing identity, refund framing, and legal exposure depend on resolving this. You cannot build acceptance criteria for a promise whose nature is undefined.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Legal/Compliance", "Customer Support", "Marketing"],
      "options": [
        "A) Frame as a guarantee with contractual refund obligation — Legal must approve revised language",
        "B) Frame as best-effort with goodwill refund — Product and CX must accept weaker customer messaging",
        "C) Frame as a 'commitment' (middle ground) — e.g., 'We aim to deliver within 2 hours. If we don't, we'll refund the express fee.' — Legal reviews for enforceability"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C. Use language like 'Express Delivery Commitment' — avoids the word 'guarantee' while still communicating a strong customer promise. The refund becomes the concrete commitment, not the delivery time itself. Legal must sign off on exact wording."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "What happens when the 15-minute pickup SLA is breached? Operations says 'we can't guarantee 2 hours,' but no escalation action is defined. Options: (a) auto-cancel the express upgrade and refund the fee, (b) escalate to dispatch supervisor, (c) continue and accept higher miss-window risk, (d) reassign to another driver.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Warehouse Operations", "Drivers"],
      "options": [
        "Auto-downgrade to standard delivery and refund express fee",
        "Reassign to next available express-eligible driver",
        "Continue with compressed window and flag for monitoring"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Reassign to next available driver. If no driver is available within an additional 5 minutes, auto-downgrade to standard and refund the express fee proactively. This preserves the customer experience without creating guaranteed failures."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "How is 'wrong address' determined, and who makes this determination? Finance says no refund for wrong addresses, but there is no defined process for adjudicating whether an address was genuinely wrong vs. hard to find. This is a high-dispute-risk area.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Customer Support", "Finance"],
      "options": [
        "Driver flags wrong address in-app with photo evidence, CS reviews",
        "System auto-detects via geocoding mismatch",
        "CS adjudicates all wrong-address claims manually"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Require driver to flag with photo evidence + GPS mismatch. CS reviews within refund processing window. Default to issuing refund if evidence is inconclusive — better to over-refund than to damage customer trust on a new premium product."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No specification for what 'real-time tracking' means technically or from a UX perspective. What is the update frequency? What happens if GPS signal is lost? Is tracking available from order placement, pickup, or only during transit? What is shown when driver is between assignments?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Drivers", "Engineering"],
      "options": [
        "Define tracking as GPS updates every 10-30 seconds, available from pickup to delivery",
        "Define tracking as map view with ETA, available from driver assignment onward"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Tracking should be available from driver assignment (not order placement — driver may not be assigned yet). GPS updates every 15 seconds during transit. Show last known location with staleness indicator if GPS drops for >60 seconds."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No definition of how 10km distance is measured (straight-line vs. driving distance), and no specification for which warehouse is selected when multiple are in range. These are critical for the eligibility check in BR-1.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Warehouse Operations", "Engineering"],
      "options": [
        "Straight-line (haversine) distance — simpler, but may include unreachable addresses",
        "Driving distance via routing API — more accurate, but adds latency and cost to eligibility checks"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Use straight-line distance for eligibility gating (fast, deterministic) but validate with driving distance estimate before confirming express availability. Select warehouse with shortest estimated driving time, not just nearest by radius."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": [
    { "violation": "Cannot finalize acceptance criteria while the guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction is unresolved.", "business_rule_broken": "The nature of the 2-hour commitment (BR-5) is undefined — is it a contractual guarantee or a best-effort target? This affects refund obligation framing, customer-facing language, and legal exposure." }
  ]
}
